reasoning: The base rate for a successful appeal of a CAS arbitral award to the Swiss Federal Tribunal (SFT) is historically extremely low, around 0.3% to 1%. Jordan Chiles filed her appeals in September 2024 and March 2025, but as of August 2025, reports indicate the decision was still pending and taking longer than expected. Even if the SFT does ultimately rule in her favor, SFT rulings generally remand the case back to CAS rather than overturning the medal decision directly. A complete overturning of the reallocation and reinstatement of Chiles before January 1, 2026, seems all but impossible given the slow pace of the SFT, the historical likelihood of success, and the short remaining window (less than 2 months from the knowledge cutoff) for potential remanded procedures. The community prediction of 1% perfectly captures this, and my estimate is the allowable minimum of 2%.
